message:
Harden extension subsystem: proper I/O error propagation, richer classification, memory-safe rationale

Extension dispatcher & hostcall exec (extension_dispatcher.rs, extensions.rs):
- Stdout/stderr reader threads now return Result<Vec<u8>, String>
  instead of silently ignoring read errors via `let _ = ...`
- Thread join failures propagate as descriptive errors ("stdout reader
  thread panicked") instead of silently returning empty bytes

Extension classification (extension_inclusion.rs):
- classify_registrations() now always scans all registration types
  before deciding category, fixing a bug where multi-registration
  extensions with index 0 being an unknown type were misclassified
- Added "registerFlag"/"registerShortcut" as Configuration category in
  the multi-category distinct-count check
- build_rationale() replaces .leak() string literals with Cow<str> to
  avoid permanent memory leaks on every call with tier-1/tier-2 args
- Added tests: unknown+known registration combo, configuration+tool
  as Multi, tier-1 rationale without leak pattern

Event loop & audit ledger (extensions_js.rs):
- Timer ID and audit sequence counters use saturating_add to prevent
  overflow/wrap at u64::MAX
- clear_timeout() now returns bool and only inserts into cancelled set
  when the timer actually exists in the heap, preventing unbounded
  growth of cancelled_timers from repeated/invalid cancel calls
- Added tests: nonexistent cancel returns false, double cancel returns
  false, timer ID saturation, audit sequence saturation

Conformance docs (extension-conformance-test-plan.json):
- Added exemplar extensions to conformance matrix cells (subagent,
  custom-provider-qwen-cli, sandbox, ssh)
- Coverage improved from 13→16 cells, 6→7 categories

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
